scope: Acumatica financial foundation setup (Implementing Basic Financials). NOT all of Acumatica: excludes Distribution/Projects/Manufacturing/FA/Payroll/CRM and the transactional layer.
source: KB 6-area sweep + live /structure sweep (local2026r1) + proven grp-mcp session experience, incl. a full blank-tenant foundation bootstrap run (2026-07-02). Full prose: OPEX/PS/setup-map/SETUP_MAP.md.

layers.documented: order/prereqs/KB gotchas below — version-stable, safe to trust.
layers.live: ALWAYS recompute per-instance via ui_get_structure(screen)/check state — never trust baked required-field lists blindly; features, licensing, and what's already configured are instance-specific.

cross_cutting_rules.rule: Numbering sequences (CS201010) gate EVERY module Preferences form. GL/AP/AR/CA Prefs mark specific numbering IDs required and won't Save without them. Seeded datasets have the predefined set; a bare tenant needs them created first.

Control accounts must be flagged on GL202500 (Control Account Module = AP|AR|TX|PO) BEFORE class/agency setup. The #1 AP/AR/TX trap. 'Account exists' != 'flagged'. Cash accounts (CA202000) EXCLUDE control accounts from the GL-account picker.
Base currency is set at company creation (CS101500) and is PERMANENT once GL102000 is saved. It becomes the Actual-ledger currency. Get it right first.
Retained Earnings + YTD Net Income (GL102000) must be type Liability and exist in the CoA first; BOTH lock after the first income/expense posting. RetEarn picker lists Liability accounts only.

SetupNotEntered = that module's Preferences form hasn't been Saved yet (singleton DACs: APSetup/ARSetup/CASetup). A bare Save (required fields default in) materializes it and unblocks the module's sub-screens AND some API reads. Probe with ui_get_structure(<PrefsScreen>).
GL201000 Generate creates periods Inactive (not postable). You MUST Open them on GL503000 (Action=Open, Process All) to post.

Set before go-live (lock after first posting): ledger type/currency (GL201500, one Actual/company); RetEarn+YTD (GL102000); AP/AR/GL numbering sequences; cash-account branch (CA202000); currency decimal precision (CM202000); segment length if values exist.

A missing tab/section = the feature is OFF on CS100000, not a bug. Never hardcode a feature list; read live CS100000. Don't disable an in-use feature (data corruption). Multiple Base Currencies, once on, blocks re-enabling other features.

Segmented-key structure is near-immutable: only add/remove segment, split, change length are supported; else contact support. A key needs >=1 segment; a full multi-segment key delete is impossible via ANY API or the UI (leave intact, do not header-delete or the last segment orphans).

grp-mcp plane by screen shape: master-detail with a grid -> classic screen_submit/screen_insert_rows. Dialog/process action classic-SOAP can't reach -> modern ui_screen_action (e.g. GL201000 Generate). Plain record/Prefs Save -> either. Deletes need allow_delete.

A clean ok/raw_len~=335 is NOT proof of persistence. Read back: run_dac_odata (header) + screen_get (detail grids). Dry-run (dry_run=true) validates a sequence without persisting, but does NOT run enum validation that happens on Save.

create_or_update_entity's PUT response echoes nested detail collections as [] even when they persisted correctly (proven: TaxReportingSettings.ReportingGroups, Tax.TaxSchedule, TaxCategory.Details, TaxZone.ApplicableTaxes all showed [] on write, all present on read-back). FIXED in grp-mcp v0.25.0+: create_or_update_entity now auto-detects this (a field you sent non-empty came back []), re-fetches by id with that field expanded, and patches the real values into the result; if the re-fetch itself fails the result carries `_unverified_details` naming the still-suspect keys. Still verify manually with get_entity(..., expand=...) on older versions or if `_unverified_details` appears.

A nested detail collection in create_or_update_entity ALWAYS APPENDS unless a row's own `id` is included - resending the identical detail array (e.g. re-saving the same ReportingGroups) creates duplicate rows every single time, it does not match-and-update by content. To update/delete an EXISTING detail row you must pass its `id` (from a get_entity fetch) - {"id":<id>, ...changed fields...} to update, {"id":<id>,"delete":true} to remove (id/delete stay bare, not {"value":...}-wrapped).

A detail row's `id` in create_or_update_entity/get_entity responses is NOT a stable key across separate requests - two consecutive GETs of the same record returned completely different `id` values for the same rows (matched by content/rowNumber). It DID remain valid when used in an action issued immediately after the fetch that produced it. Rule: fetch, then act on those exact ids right away in the same turn - never cache a detail row's id across a later, separate call.

Creating the FIRST real Vendor record (not just a VendorClass) surfaces 2 requirements the class alone won't: (1) DiscTakenAcctID ('Cash Discount Account') must be set on the vendor's class (AP201000 CashDiscountAccount) - fix at the class so all future vendors inherit it; (2) MainContact.Address.Country is mandatory, 2-letter ISO code (e.g. 'MY', not 'MYS' - check via run_dac_odata('Country')).

Modern-plane TREE controls (e.g. SM207060 EntityTree) are NOT normal data grids — ui_insert_grid_row/etc. throw a server-side null-reference against one. Call ui_select_tree_node(tree_view, node_key) FIRST (ui_screen_action tree_select param) to make a node the active row before firing a dependent action like InsertNew, or it silently no-ops (200, nothing happens, no error). The select itself needs THREE things together or it also silently no-ops: activeRowContexts, the tree columns/key echoed in controlsParams, AND viewsParams naming the node's "Selected*" context views (e.g. SelectedEndpoint/SelectedEntity/SelectedAction) — omitting any one still returns 200 with no error message. The select command name ("EnablePopulate" on SM207060) is the screen's own selection-changed handler, not a generic framework primitive — may differ per screen.

CONFIRMED LIMITATION (exhaustive live probing on 2026R1, 2026-07-06): the Company Tree (EP204061) and, by extension, the Approval Maps Steps/Rules tree (EP205015) canNOT have an arbitrary existing node RE-SELECTED as the active parent through EITHER API plane, so move / re-parent / multi-sibling / arbitrary rename+delete are NOT reliably automatable and these screens stay a browser-only task. What was tried and FAILED to establish server-side selection (CurrentWorkGroup stayed null / new node landed at root ParentWGID=0): modern /ui/screen/ activeRowContexts+controlsParams+Selected* echo (unlike SM207060's EntityTree, which DOES take it — the difference is the screen's own selection-changed handler, which EP204061 does not expose by any discoverable name); the same with the hidden root node 0 in the ancestor path; classic screen-SOAP CompanyTree.Value field-set; classic CompanyTree.Path in 4 formats ("128", "\128", "0\128", "//128"). The ONLY deterministic primitive is: AddWorkGroup nests a child under whatever node is CURRENTLY active, and a node becomes current only by having just been added+Saved in the SAME graph (no re-bootstrap) — so a pure descending chain builds, but siblings/re-parent do not. Why no browser HAR rescue: the classic /Main UI is ASPX in an iframe (its postback protocol != the modern /ui/screen/ plane grp-mcp drives), and no browser frontend uses the modern plane for this screen, so there is no real selection payload to capture. Backing DAC for READS is EPCompanyTree (WorkGroupID, Description, ParentWGID, SortOrder) — ui_read_grid(fallback_dac='EPCompanyTree') returns the full tree (the grid read alone only returns the collapsed root). To automate creation/moves, use real browser UI automation (Playwright with login + true coordinate clicks), NOT the API planes. company_tree and build_approval_map were DEFERRED on this basis (2026-07-06).

SM207060 entity-add is FULLY cracked + reproducible via the modern plane (proven live 2026-07-02, multiple entities on a fresh endpoint, each verified against swagger.json). Use the ui_tree_dialog_insert MCP tool: it runs the real UIs 5-phase sequence in one call — (1) ui_select_tree_node on ROOT#<endpoint> [caches the EntityTree control block + Selected* context viewsParams, both auto-attached to every later call]; (2) fire open_action (InsertNew) to OPEN a blank dialog; (3) Repaint with viewsParams={dialog_view:{}} to load the dialog fields; (4) set each dialog field; (5) fire open_action AGAIN with dialogCallback {dialogResult:1, viewName:dialog_view} to COMMIT the dialog (this only STAGES the node — graphIsDirty:true), then a SEPARATE Save to PERSIST. The missing separate Save + the missing Selected* viewsParams on later calls were why earlier hand-rolled attempts returned 200 with no error yet added nothing. Prereqs: pass record_key={view:Endpoint, key:{InterfaceName, GateVersion}} so the keyed primary view loads (else commit fails The Insert button is disabled); resolve the ScreenID selector first with ui_resolve_selector (search by title, pick={screenID:...} to disambiguate duplicate titles like Companies=CS1015PL[a GI, invalid source] vs CS101500[real screen]) and pass its {id,text} value straight into fields. EntityType resolves server-side at commit — omit it. This adds only the entity SHELL; adding scalar FIELDS needs a further PopulateFields dialog pass (not yet a tool; playwright/populate_all_views.js is the fallback). The generic lessons — ui_resolve_selector (any selector field, from its own /structure fieldDacName->graph + fieldList) and ui_navigate_record (select a specific keyed record) — are reusable on ANY modern-plane screen, not just SM207060.

After ui_tree_dialog_insert adds an endpoint entity SHELL (SM207060), expose its scalar FIELDS with the ui_populate_endpoint_entity_fields tool — SM207060s Populate flow (proven live 2026-07-02: ImportScenarios field_count 1->20). It: navigates the Endpoint record, selects ROOT to list entity nodes, finds the target by Title(==ObjectName), selects that ENTITY node, fires PopulateFields (opens dialog), Repaints viewsParams={PopulateFilterView:{}}, resolves the Container data-view selector [ENTITY-SCOPED — its lookup only returns THIS entitys views, so it must resolve in-session with the entity node active; ui_resolve_selector rides the active tree row onto the /grid query], sets Container, fires SelectAll, commits with dialogCallback OK, then a separate Save. Two selector gotchas cracked here, now handled generically in ui_resolve_selector: (a) some selectors (Container) omit fieldList -> columns fall back to [valueField, descriptionName]; (b) some omit fieldDacName -> graph falls back to the screen graph (get_ui_structure.screen_graph, taken from any sibling selector that has one). Data-view display names in the modern selector differ from the classic UI Object names and can REPEAT (ImportByScenario has two Details) — pass data_view_pick={mappedObject:...} to disambiguate. Adds fields from ONE top-level view; pass detail_title=<collection name> (matches the "<Collection>: <Type>[]" node title) to target a nested DETAIL collection instead of the top-level entity — same mechanism, see ui-detail-collections-cracked for the depth-2 wrinkles.

Nested DETAIL collections on an SM207060 endpoint entity are fully cracked, both CREATE and POPULATE (proven 2026-07-02, mine + an independent live browser capture, byte-matched). CREATE: ui_tree_dialog_insert targeting the parent ENTITY node (not root) with the InsertNew dialog's ObjectType="D" (Detail) or "L" (Linked) — confirmed a "<Field>: <Type>[]" node appears under the entity. SELECT (prerequisite for populate/any op on the node): a detail node is depth-2 and its tree parameters must encode the FULL ancestor chain [root, entity, None, detail], not just the immediate parent — sending only the parent returns activeRowId:null with no error (silent no-op). ui_select_tree_node's ancestor_keys=[root_node_key, entity_key] param (or ui_populate_entity_fields's detail_title=...) handles this. POPULATE: identical select→Populate→Repaint→resolve Container→SelectAll→commit→Save skeleton as the top-level-entity case. A populate that returns 0 fields is NOT necessarily a bug — two confirmed legitimate causes, both reproduced byte-identically in a live browser capture: (a) the chosen data view genuinely maps no scalar fields onto that object (0 fields, no error, on both sides); (b) the view's fields collide with a name already staged elsewhere on the endpoint, surfaced as a messageType:"error" on the commit call (e.g. "An element with the DesignID name already exists") — _ui_error already raises this as a ScreenError rather than swallowing it. If populate keeps returning 0, try a different data view, or a DIFFERENT (never-populated) detail node, before assuming the tool is broken — proven live 2026-07-02: a brand-new detail node on the SAME entity populated cleanly (Conditions view, GRPMCP2/GIFresh/WheresTest) right after an already-poisoned sibling node (Params/GIFreshParam) kept colliding on every view tried. The collision sticks to the SPECIFIC node once one field has landed there, not the entity or endpoint as a whole.

Modern UI-JSON plane essentials (learned 2026-07-02 doing SM203520 snapshot restore). (1) AUTH: the modern plane (/ui/screen/<ID>) rides the SAME login cookie as the classic SOAP Login — it is NOT separately authenticated. An unauthenticated/expired session does NOT 401 cleanly; it answers HTTP 200/302 with a {"redirect":".../Login.aspx"} body, which naively parses as "no error, empty data" -> you silently get an empty structure/grid and misattribute the cause (I wrongly blamed a maintenance lockout). _ui_error now flags that redirect as NOT AUTHENTICATED, and the modern entrypoints self-heal by calling login() if needed; through the MCP tools (async with ScreenClient) login is automatic. A goTo/other redirect is NOT an auth failure. (2) MAINTENANCE LOCKOUT (SM203510 Schedule/Stop Maintenance) does NOT block the admin who scheduled it, nor the SOAP/REST planes (per-call auth) — only non-admin forms-auth UI sessions. So you can drive a restore via the modern plane DURING the lockout as admin. (3) ROW-SCOPED ACTIONS: classic SOAP can navigate a keyed MASTER record but CANNOT select an arbitrary existing GRID row by key (RowNumber is inert) — so a "select this row, then click a toolbar button" flow (SM203520 Restore Snapshot -> importSnapshotCommand; faults "A snapshot is not selected" on SOAP) is impossible there. Use the modern-plane ui_grid_row_action tool: it makes the row active via activeRowContexts (GridActiveDataRow + the row key), fires the action, auto-answers the 302 openDialog with OK; confirm=False arms without committing; parent={view,key} scopes a tenant/master header. It returns an honest status: committed / dialog_open / redirected (a goTo like Restore->SM203510 is a hand-off to run/monitor, NOT a synchronous completion — verify the downstream effect). (4) ROUTING: call screen_capabilities(screen_id) to get the recommended plane/tool per operation shape instead of trial-and-error. Plane-by-shape: edit keyed master / bulk-append rows -> SOAP (screen_record/screen_insert_rows, also the only plane that works under lockout); select-row-then-act or dialog/process action -> modern; selector field -> ui_resolve_selector first; entity on the endpoint -> REST.

Classic screen-SOAP write footguns (probed live 2026-07-03; grp-mcp v0.36 hardens the first four). (1) ENUM values: an INVALID enum on a SOAP SET is accepted with ok:true and silently no-ops/defaults (proven: GL102000 TrialBalanceSign='Disbursement' -> stayed unchanged, ok:true). A VALID code works ('R' -> Reversed). v0.36 screen_submit now pre-validates SETs against modern-plane options and returns ok:false+validation_errors instead; pass skip_validation=true to override. (2) READ-ONLY fields: writing one returns ok:true/raw_len~335 but never persists (proven: GL102000 AllocationNumberingID, read-only while the Allocation feature is off). v0.36 rejects these up front too. Both checks are best-effort (only fire when the field is found in the modern /structure; grid columns and modern-unreachable screens skip validation). (3) TRUNCATED insert faults: a Save/insert fault used to surface as 'Inserting <X> record raised' with empty field_errors — the ' at ' stack-trace boundary was chopping '...raised at LEAST one error. Please review.' mid-sentence; v0.36 fixes the boundary AND, when no field detail is available, attaches required_fields + fields_you_set as a hint. (4) FILTER conditions (screen_get): only the 'condition' key with an Acumatica name was honored; an 'op' key or an operator symbol ('>=') was silently ignored, defaulting to Equals and returning WRONG rows (proven: Account>='300000' returned 1 not 4). v0.36 accepts operator aliases (>=,>,<=,<,=,!=,contains,...) via 'op' OR 'condition' and REJECTS unknown keys/conditions loudly. (5) GRID KEY-EDIT (unfixed platform footgun): setting a grid KEY field via classic SOAP (e.g. GL202500 AccountRecords.Account) is read as 'change row 1's key' -> pops 'Are you sure you want to change the key?'; a reflexive auto_answer='Yes' RENAMES the wrong row. Use modern ui_update_grid_row (key-addressed) for grid edits; screen_capabilities warns. (6) A clean ok/raw_len~335 STILL is not proof of persistence for anything the above validation can't see (grid rows, detail collections) -> read back (run_dac_odata + screen_get). (7) MODERN-PLANE PARITY (v0.37): the same silent enum/read-only drop exists on the modern plane and is now guarded there too — ui_screen_action pre-validates set_fields (rejects read-only/invalid-enum, ok:false+validation_errors), COERCES an enum display label to its option value (pass 'Reversed' OR 'R'), and resolves a bare field name to its view; returns graph_is_dirty and (verify=true) re-reads after Save. ui_preflight dry-runs the same checks without writing. Grid-CELL writes are ALSO validated as of v0.38: ui_insert_grid_row/ui_update_grid_row check each cell against the grid's live column meta — a read-only cell (allowUpdate=false) or invalid enum is REFUSED (ok:false+validation_errors), an enum display label is coerced to its stored value (Type 'Expense'==='E'). Column meta comes from the grid read's columns (carry allowUpdate + valueItems); a grid read of an EMPTY grid returns 0 columns, so the meta falls back to the /structure controlsData.<grid>.columns (proven live: GL102000 SetupApproval, 0-row grid, recovered 5 cols). skip_validation=true bypasses; unknown columns are passed through (never blocked). So enum/read-only silent-drop is now guarded on ALL write shapes (SOAP submit, modern form fields, modern grid cells).

Modern UI-JSON plane surface (probed live 2026-07-03; the plane is UNDOCUMENTED — the KB's 'Screen-Based Web Services API' is the CLASSIC SOAP plane, not this one, so live probing is the only source). It has essentially THREE routes: POST /t/<Tenant>/ui/screen/<ID> (commands+field data+grids, the workhorse), GET .../structure (metadata: views/fields/options/readonly + grids), POST /ui/screen/<ID>/grid (NO tenant prefix — selector/lookup sub-queries). Everything else is a screen ACTION, not a route: sitemap/menu/report/export/navigation/files all 404 as GET. Tools that wrap useful action patterns: ui_screen_action (set fields + fire action), ui_run_process (v0.39 — fire Process/ProcessAll and drive to completion; SMALL batches finish synchronously in one call [verified GL503000], a large one opens a processing dialog polled via actionCloseProcessing), ui_lookup (v0.39 — multi-row reference-table search via any selector field, rides ui_resolve_selector), ui_preflight (dry-run validate). NOT worth wrapping on this plane: reports (runReport is stateful + 500s cold; the DOCUMENTED path is GI/DAC OData via run_generic_inquiry/run_dac_odata/run_report). 'Load Records from File' (grid UploadFileAction) is a MULTI-step upload+2-dialog flow (KB: File Upload -> Choose File -> Upload -> Common Settings null-value/culture -> load) — needs a real browser network capture to crack safely, do NOT guess the multipart handshake.

Modern UI-JSON protocol facts CONFIRMED against Acumatica's own T290 ModernUI dev training (2025R2), 2026-07-03 — use these instead of re-deriving. (1) ACTION NAMES: the name fired over the JSON protocol == the backend PXAction field name (custom actions = PascalCase, e.g. 'OpenInvoice'). STANDARD GRID-TOOLBAR buttons have FIXED lowercase names: refresh (Refresh), insert (Add Row), delete (Delete Row), adjust (Fit to Screen), filter (Filter Settings), import (Load Records from File), exportToExcel (Export to Excel). (2) FIELD STATE: fieldState.enabled=false is THE disabled/read-only signal (get_ui_structure already parses enabled + readOnly). Only a field's `value` round-trips to the server; `config` properties are browser-only and are overwritten server-side each round trip. (3) PAYLOAD vs RESPONSE naming (both correct in grp-mcp, don't 'fix'): the REQUEST payload carries viewsParams (CreateSingle views) + controlsParams (CreateCollection views/controls to reload) + data (field changes -> PXCache); the RESPONSE carries controlsData (the actual grid rows/cells) + fieldStates + actionStates. (4) SELECTOR: a selector's controlConfig.suggester.descriptionName names the human-readable description column returned by the lookup (relevant to ui_resolve_selector/ui_lookup parsing). (5) CAPTURE TECHNIQUE (for cracking the multi-step import/export handshakes): the modern UI's Network tab names the useful requests by FORM ID; the first GET is 'structure' (cached); POSTs (form-ID-named, never cached) carry the change 'data'. Export/Import a HAR from the Network tab toolbar to record the exact upload/download requests. exportToExcel + import both return a JSON envelope first (a token/URL handshake, NOT a one-shot file) — confirmed live 2026-07-03 (exportToExcel -> JSON, not an xlsx) — so both need a HAR capture to implement, not guesswork. (6) PROCESSING FORMS: a PXProcessingBase<Table> data view gives Process/ProcessAll buttons + an unbound 'Selected' Boolean column for per-row selection; the training does NOT document the long-running/progress POST protocol (defers externally) — so ui_run_process's async-poll branch remains reverse-engineered (sync path verified).

When the classic screen-SOAP plane CAN'T do it, route to the modern UI-JSON plane (learned 2026-07-03). (a) SPARSE SCHEMA: some screens expose almost nothing via screen_get_schema (e.g. CS211000 Reason Code shows only the ReasonCodeID selector — Description/Usage/SubMask/Account are absent), so a SOAP insert is impossible; drive creation via the modern plane (ui_screen_action / ui_insert_grid_row). (b) DELETE distinction: pure inline-GRID screens have no SOAP Delete ('field Delete not found', e.g. CA203000) -> use ui_delete_grid_row; but form-MASTER screens with First/Prev/Next/Last nav (CS211000, IN202000, AP303000) DO support classic SOAP delete. (c) REPORTS + ATTACHMENTS are contract-REST only (run_report/attach_file) and 404 on a custom endpoint without the files route — read data from inquiry screens/GIs instead when a report path is needed. Rule of thumb: screen_capabilities(screen_id) recommends the plane per operation; a SOAP 'not found'/sparse-schema is a routing signal, not a dead end.

Standing up a TRULY BLANK tenant (proven live 2026-07-02, blank 2026R1 -> multibranch company): working order = Features (enable_features stage + activate_features) -> Company (CS101500) -> Branches (CS102000) -> Ledger (REST) -> rest of canonical order. Three bootstrap traps: (1) BASE CURRENCY IS CREATED INLINE BY THE COMPANY SAVE, not beforehand - creating a Currency standalone (CM202000 / 'Currency' entity) on a blank tenant fails PXSetupNotEntered ('Company Branches form') because CurrencyMaint itself needs a company; instead TYPE the new base currency code into OrganizationView.BaseCuryID on CS101500 (its options list is empty - that's fine) and the company save creates the currency. Base currency stays changeable until GL102000 saves. (2) The company save does NOT create the Actual ledger even though CreateLedgerView.LedgerCD looks required - create it separately via create_or_update_entity('Ledger', {LedgerID, Type:'Actual', Currency, Companies:[{Company:<CD>}]}) on the Default endpoint; the nested Companies row makes the link (Organization.ActualLedgerID gets set). (3) GL202000/AccountClass (and other GL reads) 500 with PXSetupNotEnteredException[PX.Objects.GL.Branch] until a company + branch RECORD exists - it wants the records, not GL prefs; creating the branches clears it. Also: Branch/$adHocSchema 403 just means the Multibranch feature is still off.

RELIABLE headless way to build a COMPREHENSIVE web-service endpoint (every field incl. tab/override-gated ones the SM207060 PopulateFields dialog structurally can't reach): transplant a proven <EntityEndpoint> project.xml - retarget <Endpoint name=.. version=..> + <ExtendsEndpoint>, then import_customization(replace=True) + publish_customization. GOTCHA (cost an hour): every detail-collection field typed 'XxxLines[]' needs its sibling <Detail name="XxxLines"> type-definition element (sits under <Endpoint> after the TopLevelEntity blocks) included too - dropping them passes static validation but the publish DB-apply fails fast with 'The given key was not present in the dictionary'. Publish signature: SUCCESS = long site recompile (~1-3 min; publish_customization v0.32+ is non-blocking - poll publish_status); FAILURE returns fast with an error log, so bisecting a bad project.xml is cheap. Endpoint names: letters/digits/_/. only - no hyphens, no leading digit. Singleton config views (CS100000 Features, GL101000, GL102000, the period/calendar process screens) CANNOT be endpoint entities (EDM generation breaks) - drive those with the recipe tools (enable_features, create_financial_calendar, set_gl_preferences, generate_master_calendar, manage_financial_periods, set_segment_value) instead. THREE MORE proven facts (2026-07-03, building grp_mcp v2): (1) INHERITED-ENTITY EXTENSION - an extended endpoint ADDS fields to an entity inherited from its base by declaring <TopLevelEntity name="<SameName>" screen="<Screen>"> containing ONLY the new Fields+Mappings (proven: Default's Account 19->21 fields, gaining ControlAccountModule + AllowManualEntry - the two UI-only control-account trap columns, now API-settable). (2) The publish log line 'EntityEndpoint ...(skipped, already applied)' is a RED HERRING - the endpoint changes DID apply; verify via swagger/get_entity_schema(refresh=true), don't re-publish. (3) publishBegin itself can exceed 60s on a COLD site - grp-mcp v0.35+ runs it inside the background job (publish_status phase 'begin'), so a client-side timeout no longer loses the job.

CS100000.name: Enable/Disable Features
CS100000.purpose: Turn features on/off; gates company types, ledgers, and every downstream module.
prerequisites: none (step 1); production: activate license (SM201510) after enabling
required_fields: Standard Financials group (only hard requirement for basic financials)
gotchas: Modify->tick->Enable flow; Enable recompiles the site (~1-3 min)
Per company type add: no-branches=default; branches-no-balancing=+Multibranch; branches-balancing=+Multibranch+Multicompany+Advanced Financials+Inter-Branch
Multiple Base Currencies is a trap-door (blocks re-enabling others)
Advanced Financials not in Standard edition

ACTIVATE via the MODERN plane only: classic SOAP RequestValidation replays every feature field and NREs ('Object reference not set ... ProjectAccounting'), leaving ActivationStatus stuck at Pending Activation. Fire the modern command 'requestValidation' (lowercase) - it returns a reloadPage redirect as the recompile starts. activate_features v0.33+ does this automatically
Staging dependency order: Subaccounts won't tick until Advanced Financials is already ON - stage Advanced Financials first, then Subaccounts in a second enable_features call
CS100000.plane: enable_features (SOAP staging; split dependent features across 2 calls) + activate_features (v0.33+: modern-plane requestValidation, non-blocking - poll activate_features_status until ActivationStatus=Validated)

CS101500.name: Companies (+ Actual Ledger)
CS101500.purpose: Create the legal entity, set base currency, and create/link the Actual ledger.
gotchas: Base currency PERMANENT after GL102000 saved; Create Ledger uses it (set first)
One Actual ledger/company
The ledger LINK lives in grid OrganizationLedgerLinkWithLedgerSelect - a bare ledger row is NOT linked; Organization.ActualLedgerID stays null until linked

Blank-tenant create that WORKS (proven 2026-07-02): modern ui_screen_action('CS101500', action='Save', set_fields=[...]) on the fresh new-record graph - BAccount.AcctCD + BAccount.AcctName, OrganizationView.OrganizationType (enum WithoutBranches|NotBalancing; UI label 'With Branches' = NotBalancing), OrganizationView.BaseCuryID (TYPE the code even though the options list is empty - on a blank tenant the save CREATES the currency), CurrentBAccount.LegalName (REQUIRED), DefAddress.CountryID AND DefLocationAddress.CountryID (both REQUIRED; country ref data is preloaded)

The company save does NOT create the Actual ledger even though CreateLedgerView.LedgerCD looks required - easiest is create_or_update_entity('Ledger', {LedgerID, Type:'Actual', Currency, Companies:[{Company:<CD>}]}) which also makes the link (sets Organization.ActualLedgerID)
CS101500.plane: modern ui_screen_action Save (proven on a blank tenant) or screen_submit; ledger+link via create_or_update_entity('Ledger') REST

CS102000.name: Branches
CS102000.purpose: Define each branch of a multibranch company.
gotchas: A With-Branches company needs >=1 branch
Without-Branches auto-creates a read-only branch (same ID as company) - never create it
Branch does not own a ledger (Ledgers tab read-only)
Balancing needs GL201500 Branch Accounting + GL101010 mapping (post-foundation)

Proven create (2026-07-02): screen_record('CS102000', key_field='BranchSummary.BranchID', key_value=<ID>, insert=True, fields={BranchName, Company:<CD>, 'MainAddress.Country':<ISO2>, LegalName}) - QUALIFY the key as BranchSummary.BranchID, bare 'BranchID' is ambiguous (collides with SpecifyNewID)
$adHocSchema 403 on the Branch entity = Multibranch feature still OFF, not a permissions problem

CS201010.name: Numbering Sequences
CS201010.purpose: Auto-generate document IDs (GL batches, invoices, bills, payments) and auto-numbered key segments. Do EARLY - gates every module Prefs.
required_fields: one subsequence: Start Number, End Number, Numbering Step
gotchas: Start/End/Warning are alphanumeric strings, same length (<=15), same prefix; keep leading zeros (000001); End>=Start, Warning>=Start
For an auto-number segment the sequence length must equal the segment length (single-segment key may be shorter)

CS202000.name: Segment Keys
CS202000.purpose: Define identifier structure (ACCOUNT, SUBACCOUNT, CASHACCOUNT keys). Set ACCOUNT key length BEFORE CoA.
prerequisites: numbering sequence first ONLY if an auto-number segment
feature gates key availability (SUBACCOUNT needs Subaccounts feature)
gotchas: total segment length <= Max Length
only ONE auto-number segment/key, Edit Mask must be Unicode
1-segment key can't turn Validate off
don't change Prompt Character after identifiers exist
structure changes post-creation limited to add/remove segment, split, change length; length change rejected if segment values exist
multi-segment full delete impossible
header nav uses descriptor {set} NOT flat {key}

CS203000.name: Segment Values
CS203000.purpose: Populate allowable values for validated segments before users create identifiers.
gotchas: case auto-converted + checked vs Edit Mask
NO spaces in SUBACCOUNT values (treated as report wildcards)
3-part grid key (DimensionID,SegmentID,Value)
write via descriptor {set} header-nav (flat {key} silently lands in default segment)

GL101000.name: Financial Year
GL101000.purpose: Define the first financial year, period type, adjustment period.
gotchas: CANNOT post before the first period's start (define first year = year before go-live for opening balances)
Has Adjustment Period settable for all years only here
Create Periods unavailable for week-based types
one-time; structure meant to be permanent

GL201000.name: Master Financial Calendar
GL201000.purpose: Generate financial periods for a year range (created Inactive).
gotchas: periods generated INACTIVE - not postable until Opened (GL503000)
the Generate action is CLASSIC-SOAP-DEAD (silent no-op) - MUST use the modern /ui/screen/ plane
From Year can precede current first year
FA-enabled adds date-match validation

GL503000.name: Manage Financial Periods
GL503000.purpose: Open generated (Inactive) periods so they become postable.
prerequisites: periods generated on GL201000 (exist as Inactive)
gotchas: MANDATORY to post: Inactive->Open via Action=Open + Process All
multiple open periods OK
Centralized Period Mgmt OFF -> open per company

GL201500.name: Ledgers
GL201500.purpose: Create the Actual ledger (usually done via CS101500 Create Ledger).
required_fields: Currency (auto=tenant cury unless Multiple Base Currencies)
gotchas: one Actual/company
type/currency LOCK after first posting; can't delete once entries exist

GL202000.name: Account Classes
GL202000.purpose: Optional grouping of accounts for reporting. Predefined classes exist.
prerequisites: none; do before CoA if custom classes wanted
gotchas: ID can't change once assigned
delete only if unused
group same-type accounts

read/$adHocSchema 500 with PXSetupNotEnteredException[PX.Objects.GL.Branch] on a fresh instance = a company + branch RECORD must exist first (not GL prefs) - create CS101500/CS102000 records and the read clears

GL202500.name: Chart of Accounts
GL202500.purpose: Create the account structure. Must include Net Income + Retained Earnings (Liability).
gotchas: include Net Income + Retained Earnings, both LIABILITY (sample 33000/32000)
set Control Account Module = AP/AR/TX/PO on the relevant accounts NOW (trap)
ControlAccountModule enum = SHORT codes 'AP'/'AR'/'TX' (rejects labels like 'Accounts Payable')
control accounts via screen_submit GL202500 grid: new_row + set ControlAccountModule (chart_of_accounts recipe can't set it)
type change post-posting limited to Income<->Expense / Asset<->Liability (Financial Supervisor)
posted accounts can't be deleted (deactivate)

GL102000.name: General Ledger Preferences
GL102000.purpose: Set system accounts + posting flags. HARD GATE: needs RetEarn+NetIncome in CoA first.
prerequisites: CoA with Retained Earnings + YTD Net Income (Liability)
gotchas: RetEarn+YTD LOCK after first income/expense posting
RetEarn picker = Liability accounts only
Sign of Trial Balance before importing a TB
Auto-Post on Release default ON

CS206500.name: Credit Terms
CS206500.purpose: Reusable payment terms (shared AP+AR). Before any vendor/customer class.
gotchas: cash discount only for Single installment
Visible To governs where terms appear (wrong value = 'terms missing from class dropdown')

CA101000.name: Cash Management Preferences
CA101000.purpose: CA global settings. Saving materializes CASetup and unblocks CA202000/CA203000.
prerequisites: GL complete (Company+Actual Ledger, CoA, GL Prefs)
cash-in-transit GL account exists in CoA
gotchas: Cash-in-Transit Account is the ONLY mandatory business setting
Auto-Post on Release default ON; consider clearing Hold Transactions on Entry
seeded CA sequences: BATCH/CATRAN/CATRANSFER/CABATCH/CABANKSTMT/CARECON

CA202000.name: Cash Accounts
CA202000.purpose: Create a cash account and link it to a GL account.
prerequisites: target GL account exists (not a control account)
gotchas: CONTROL accounts EXCLUDED from the Account picker (#1 'account not listed')
one cash account per (Branch+Subaccount+GL account)
branch LOCKS after posting
verify GL202500 shows the read-only Cash Account flag
run Recalculation of Cash Account Balances if the GL account had prior postings
friendly 'CashAccount'/'Description' are ambiguous -> qualify CashAccountSummary.*

CA203000.name: Entry Types
prerequisites: cash accounts + offset GL accounts exist
gotchas: CA-module type REQUIRES a Default Offset Account (expense for Disbursement, asset for Receipt); AP/AR types don't
DrCr stored as C (disbursement) / D (receipt)
associate each type to a cash account on CA202000 Entry Types tab

CA204000.name: Payment Methods
CA204000.purpose: How money moves in/out; bind to allowed cash accounts.
gotchas: predefined methods usually exist (CHECK/CASH/ACH...) - adjust, don't recreate
Credit Card/EFT: Integrated Processing <-> Require Card Number must both be on or both off
means-of-payment options feature-gated
associate to a cash account on CA202000 PaymentMethods tab

AR202800.name: Statement Cycles
AR202800.purpose: Schedules for customer statements + aging periods. Before AR customer class.
prerequisites: master calendar if Schedule Type = End of Financial Period
required_fields: aging periods (AgeDays1/2/3 e.g. 30/60/90 - HARD required, Save fails without)
gotchas: AR-only; no AP equivalent
required before AR201000 Customer Class (class carries a Statement Cycle ID)
aging bucket days are mandatory but dry_run MASKS it (dry_run drops Save) - a clean dry_run here does NOT mean the real Save will pass
ScheduleType 'End of Month' avoids needing a day-of-month

AP201000.name: Vendor Classes
AP201000.purpose: Default GL accounts/terms/method for vendors. Create DEFAULT first.
gotchas: GL Accounts tab load-bearing: AP/Prepayment/Retainage must be Control Account Module = AP (PO Accrual = PO) on GL202500
Description is ambiguous (repeats in Mapping/LienWaiver tabs) - qualify VendorClassRecord.Description
class changes don't retro-update existing vendors

CashDiscountAccount (DiscTakenAcctID) not required to SAVE the class, but IS required the moment you create a real Vendor from it - set it here up front (see first-vendor-needs-discount-acct-and-address) or the first vendor creation 422s

AP101000.name: Accounts Payable Preferences
AP101000.purpose: AP subledger settings. Bare Save materializes APSetup.
prerequisites: Company+Ledger -> CoA -> GL Prefs -> CA202000 Cash Accounts -> CA204000 Payment Methods -> Credit Terms -> DEFAULT vendor class
gotchas: AP needs Cash Accounts (unlike AR)
numbering sequences immutable post-production
Save once to clear SetupNotEntered
AP101000.verify: ui_get_structure('AP101000') reachable; enter a vendor works

AR201000.name: Customer Classes
AR201000.purpose: Default GL accounts/terms/cycle for customers. Create DEFAULT first.
gotchas: GL Accounts tab: AR/Prepayment/Retainage must be Control Account Module = AR on GL202500
needs a SALES/Income account in CoA (SalesAccount required) - default demo CoA has none, create e.g. 40000 first
Description ambiguous - qualify CustomerClassRecord.Description
needs BOTH Credit Terms + a Statement Cycle (2 deps vs AP's 1)
dunning levels set at class level only

AR101000.name: Accounts Receivable Preferences
AR101000.purpose: AR subledger settings. Bare Save materializes ARSetup.
prerequisites: Company+Ledger -> CoA -> GL Prefs -> CA204000 Payment Methods -> Credit Terms -> DEFAULT customer class (AR does NOT need Cash Accounts)
gotchas: Age Credits ties to AR202800 aging
numbering sequences immutable post-production
Save once to clear SetupNotEntered
AR101000.verify: ui_get_structure('AR101000') reachable; enter a customer works

TX205100.name: Reporting Settings
TX205100.purpose: Tax report lines + Input/Output reporting groups per agency.
required_fields: for full tax-RETURN generation (not required just to apply tax to transactions): exactly one report line flagged Net Tax - that's the ReportLine grid on the classic screen, NOT exposed on the contract API, needs screen_submit/ui_screen_action(TX205100) if you need statutory return output
gotchas: no Net Tax line = no bill generated on report release (report-generation feature only; a Tax works on transactions without it)
each tax's Schedule tab needs a Reporting Group -> groups must exist first
MUCH SIMPLER than originally mapped: create_or_update_entity('TaxReportingSettings', {TaxAgency, ReportingGroups:[{Name,GroupType:'Output'|'Input'}]}) - no screen_submit needed for the groups
TX205100.plane: create_or_update_entity (contract REST; ReportLine/Bucket grids need screen_submit only if pursuing full tax-return generation)
TX205100.verify: get_entity('TaxReportingSettings', filter="TaxAgency eq '<id>'", expand='ReportingGroups') - list GET omits details, must fetch by key

TX205000.name: Taxes
prerequisites: GL TX-control accounts (24100 Tax Payable Liability/Control=TX, 17000 Tax Claimable Asset/Control=TX, 65100 Tax Expense)
Tax Agency vendor (AP303000, Vendor is Tax Agency)
required_fields: Schedule tab: rate + Reporting Group + StartDate
gotchas: SetupNotEntered until Tax Prefs/agency exist
VAT subtypes need VAT Reporting feature
Tax Agency is the Vendor.VendorIsTaxAgency flag on Vendors (AP303000/Vendor entity) - no standalone screen
TaxClaimableAccount stays empty for TaxType=Sales (input-credit is a VAT concept, not a bug)
MUCH SIMPLER than mapped: create_or_update_entity('Tax', {TaxID,TaxType,TaxAgency,TaxPayableAccount,TaxExpenseAccount,TaxSchedule:[{StartDate,TaxRate,ReportingGroup}]}) via contract REST

TX205500.name: Tax Categories
TX205500.purpose: Group taxes by good/service type; assigned to items.
gotchas: Exclude Listed Taxes inverts membership
tax applies = intersection of item's Tax Category AND customer/vendor's Tax Zone
on contract REST: create_or_update_entity('TaxCategory', {TaxCategoryID,Details:[{TaxID}]})

TX206000.name: Tax Zones
TX206000.purpose: Group taxes by territory; assigned to customers/vendors/branches.
required_fields: Default Tax Category (recommended; not on the contract entity - UI/screen_submit only if needed)
gotchas: overlap prevented (country/state/postal)
tax applies = category ∩ zone
on contract REST: create_or_update_entity('TaxZone', {TaxZoneID,ApplicableTaxes:[{TaxID}]})

TX103000.name: Tax Preferences
TX103000.purpose: Tax subledger prefs: adjustment numbering + rounding gain/loss accounts.
gotchas: rounding gain/loss accounts become MANDATORY if Net/Gross Entry Mode feature is on

CM202000.name: Currencies
CM202000.purpose: Activate currencies + configure gain/loss accounts. Base currency is set at CS101500, not here.
prerequisites: GL gain/loss accounts exist (for foreign currencies)
gotchas: single-currency instance needs NO CM setup
Decimal Precision LOCKS after posting
base currency permanent (set at company creation)

on a BLANK tenant even the BASE currency cannot be created here first - CurrencyMaint itself fails PXSetupNotEntered ('Company Branches form') until a company exists; the base currency is created INLINE by the CS101500 company save (see blank-tenant-bootstrap)

CM201000.name: Currency Rate Types
CM201000.purpose: Rate types governing how exchange rates apply. SPOT auto-created.
gotchas: SPOT auto-created with base currency; custom types optional

CM301000.name: Currency Rates
gotchas: rates are DIRECTIONAL - define BOTH directions (EUR->USD != USD->EUR; reciprocal not auto-usable)
